steps.title: Introduce the examples
steps.content: The main clause is "Exemplars of Stigler's law abound." What follows is a list of examples from philosophy, mathematics, and fluid mechanics, so the punctuation must introduce that list while keeping the fields parallel.

steps.title: Preserve the parallel list
steps.content: Choice A strands "in philosophy" before a semicolon. Choice C treats "in philosophy" like a parenthetical aside instead of the first list item. Choice D puts the colon after only the philosophy field, separating it from the parallel series. The colon after the complete main clause correctly introduces the examples. The answer is B.
